title: peucedanum payapicum
intro: Peucedanum payapicum, also known as Thai Angelica, is a plant species in the carrot family native to Thailand. It has been traditionally used in Thai medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in digestion, as well as possessing antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Peucedanum payapicum is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil for herbal remedies, with its extracts also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
